channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Build Better AI Agents: 5 Developer Tips from the Agent Bake-Off
item.description: The Google Cloud AI Agent Bake-Off emphasizes the need for a modular, multi-agent architecture in AI development. It provides five tips for developers to enhance AI agents, focusing on specialized sub-agents and deterministic code to improve reliability.

item.title: Get ready for Google I/O: Livestream schedule revealed
item.description: Google I/O is set for May 19-20, featuring major updates in AI and other technologies. The event will include a keynote on the 'agentic era' and showcase new tools for automating workflows.

item.title: I tested every 'allergy-friendly' smart home gadget - these 6 actually keep the pollen out
item.description: A review of allergy-friendly smart home gadgets reveals six effective devices that help combat pollen during spring. The author shares personal experiences of how these gadgets improved their home environment.

item.title: Oracle taps Bloom for 2.8 GW of fuel cells to keep datacenter binge going
item.description: Oracle has expanded its partnership with Bloom Energy to provide up to 2.8 GW of fuel cell systems for its datacenter expansion. This move addresses energy needs amid slow grid connections and limited turbine availability.

item.title: California ghost-gun bill wants 3D printers to play cop, EFF says
item.description: A proposed California bill aims to hold 3D printer manufacturers accountable for preventing the production of ghost guns, raising concerns among digital rights activists about potential restrictions on open-source tools.

item.title: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description: GitHub announced updates to its secret scanning features, enhancing detection coverage and developer experience. These improvements aim to strengthen security measures for developers.

item.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
item.description: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to implant its first sensor in a human brain, potentially aiding in the treatment of neurological conditions through electrical stimulation.

item.title: GitHub invokes spirit of Phabricator with preview of Stacked PRs
item.description: GitHub has introduced Stacked PRs, a feature that allows developers to break large code changes into smaller, manageable pull requests, improving the review process.

item.title: Chrome's new 'Skills' update lets you save AI prompts now - for one-click reuse
item.description: Google's new 'Skills' update for Chrome allows users to save and reuse AI prompts for common tasks, streamlining user interactions across websites.

item.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
item.description: The 'Skills' feature in Chrome enables users to save their favorite AI prompts for easy reuse, building on the browser's integration with Gemini.

item.title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
item.description: Google Chrome has introduced AI-powered 'Skills' in its Gemini sidebar, allowing users to optimize recipes and summarize YouTube videos. This feature enhances user experience by integrating AI tools directly into the browser.

item.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
item.description: A video demonstration showcases how to turn AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome, highlighting the ease of use and functionality of the new Skills feature. This visual guide aims to help users leverage AI capabilities efficiently.

item.title: How to share audio from your Android phone to multiple earbuds (and why it's genius)
item.description: Android's audio sharing feature allows two users to listen to audio through separate earbuds, enhancing the listening experience for shared content. This feature is particularly useful for couples or friends sharing media.

item.title: Physicist reckons two-button calculator can do all elementary math
item.description: A physicist proposes that a two-button calculator could perform all elementary math operations, suggesting a simpler approach to complex calculations. This idea challenges conventional methods and could simplify educational tools.

item.title: SBOM exports are now computed asynchronously
item.description: GitHub has updated its Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) exports to operate asynchronously, improving efficiency when exporting dependency graphs. This change enhances the user experience for developers managing software dependencies.

item.title: How to use Google Messages' new Trash feature to recover texts you accidentally deleted
item.description: Google Messages has introduced a new Trash feature that allows users to recover accidentally deleted texts, adding a layer of data protection and user convenience. This feature aims to enhance user satisfaction with messaging.

item.title: Amazon pays $11.5B to satisfy satellite-envy while cowering in Musk's shadow
item.description: Amazon has acquired Globalstar for $11.5 billion to expand its satellite capabilities, aiming to compete with Elon Musk's Starlink. This deal includes operational satellites and technology that could enhance Amazon's connectivity services.

item.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is set to host industry leaders from TDK Ventures and Replit, providing networking opportunities and insights into current tech trends. The event is scheduled for April 30, with tickets still available.

item.title: Why the Apple Watch's 20-minute calibration test is worth your time - especially if you're data curious
item.description: Calibrating the Apple Watch for 20 minutes can significantly improve the accuracy of health data during workouts. This process is recommended for users seeking personalized fitness insights.

item.title: Model selection for Claude and Codex agents on github.com
item.description: GitHub now allows users to select models for Claude and Codex coding agents, similar to the Copilot cloud agent. This feature provides developers with more control over their coding assistance tools.

item.title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
item.description: Anthropic has publicly opposed an Illinois bill that would protect AI labs from liability in cases of mass deaths and financial disasters, a stance that contrasts with OpenAI's support for the bill. This disagreement highlights the ongoing debate about accountability in AI development.

item.title: How exposed is your code? Find out in minutes—for free
item.description: GitHub has launched a free Code Security Risk Assessment tool that allows organizations to quickly identify vulnerabilities in their code. This initiative aims to enhance security awareness and proactive measures in software development.

item.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
item.description: Google has introduced its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature in India, enabling users to link their Google accounts for personalized responses. This feature aims to enhance user experience by providing tailored information.

item.title: I'm ready for a foldable iPhone, but only if Apple does this right
item.description: A tech enthusiast discusses the potential for a foldable iPhone, emphasizing the need for improved multitasking capabilities to ensure its success. The article reflects on current limitations and user expectations.

item.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
item.description: After facing two removals from the App Store, the vibe-coding app Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to support mobile development. This move aims to enhance user experience and functionality.

item.title: Meta is selling refurbished Ray-Bans for as low as $197 right now - but they're going fast
item.description: Meta is currently selling refurbished Ray-Ban smart glasses at discounted prices, with models including Wayfarer and Headliner. These glasses come with a one-year warranty, appealing to budget-conscious consumers.

item.title: AWS and Hopkins Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
item.description: AWS and Johns Hopkins Engineering have developed a new database for AI and machine learning focused on antibody design. This resource leverages a diverse dataset to improve the evaluation of AI-guided antibody development.

item.title: Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’
item.description: Google's Chrome browser now allows users to save AI prompts as 'Skills' for repeated use across multiple tabs. This feature aims to streamline workflows and enhance productivity for users engaging with AI.

item.title: No honor among thieves as 0APT threatens rival ransomware gang Krybit
item.description: The ransomware gang 0APT has threatened to expose members of rival gang Krybit, indicating internal conflicts within the cybercrime community. This rivalry showcases the chaotic dynamics of the ransomware landscape.

item.title: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now Generally Available
item.description: Amazon has made its Spring AI SDK for Bedrock AgentCore generally available, allowing developers to create production-ready AI agents. This open-source library enhances the capabilities of AI applications within the Spring framework.

item.title: The Download: the state of AI, and protecting bears with drones
item.description: The Download newsletter discusses the current chaotic state of AI, highlighting contrasting views on its impact and potential. It reflects the ongoing debate about AI's role in society and the economy.

item.title: GitHub Code Quality: Improvements to standard findings in public preview
item.description: GitHub has improved its Code Quality feature, making it easier for users to navigate and address findings in their repositories. This update aims to enhance reliability and performance in coding projects.

item.title: The Evolution of Artificial Intelligence: From Handwritten Rules to Generative and Agentic Machines
item.description: This article explores the evolution of AI from its early rule-based systems to today's generative and agentic machines. It emphasizes the rich history of AI beyond the current buzzword culture.

item.title: Anthropic says its $20M donation to Public First Action can't be "used to influence federal elections" and is to educate the public on AI policy (Veronica Irwin/Transformer)
item.description: Anthropic has donated $20 million to Public First Action, clarifying that the funds cannot be used for influencing federal elections but are intended for public education on AI policy.

item.title: Oracle pops 11%, leading bounce back rally in software stocks
item.description: Oracle's stock has rebounded by 11% following a significant decline due to concerns over AI disruption. This rally reflects investor confidence in the software sector's recovery.

item.title: A Netflix accessory app keeps hitting No. 1 in the App Store, beating out ChatGPT
item.description: A Netflix Game Controller app has surged to the top of the App Store, outperforming ChatGPT. This trend suggests a growing interest in gaming as part of Netflix's strategy.

item.title: Someone Bought 30 WordPress Plugins and Planted a Backdoor in All of Them
item.description: A supply chain attack was discovered in 30 WordPress plugins, where a backdoor was planted by a new owner of a trusted plugin. This raises concerns about security in widely used software.

item.title: Roblox Will Roll Out Age-Based Accounts Amid Child-Safety Push
item.description: Roblox is implementing age-based accounts to enhance child safety, introducing three age tiers and improved parental controls. This move aims to create a safer gaming environment for children.

item.title: orz.http.server 0.1.0
item.description: A new minimal HTTP server version 0.1.0 has been released, featuring Cross-Origin isolation headers for high-resolution timers. This update is aimed at developers needing enhanced performance.

item.title: Anthropic Claude Mythos: Serious Threat or Overhyped? AI Security Institute Weighs In
item.description: The AI Security Institute has raised concerns about Claude Mythos, suggesting it could pose a significant cybersecurity threat based on early testing results. This highlights the need for vigilance in AI security.

item.title: Motorola Razr Ultra reportedly gets a bigger battery, but the same chip as last year
item.description: The Motorola Razr Ultra is set to launch with a larger battery but will use the same chip as its predecessor, indicating minimal upgrades. This suggests Motorola is focusing on battery life rather than performance enhancements.

item.title: rein-harness added to PyPI
item.description: ReIN, an open-source agentic coding runtime, has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), allowing developers to harness their code more effectively. This addition aims to enhance coding efficiency and flexibility.

item.title: OpenAI rotates macOS certs after Axios attack hit code-signing workflow
item.description: OpenAI is rotating its potentially compromised macOS code-signing certificates following a supply chain attack that exploited a malicious Axios package. This move aims to secure their development environment and prevent further vulnerabilities.

item.title: Mario Adorf Dead: 'Tin Drum,' 'Winnetou' Actor Was 95
item.description: Mario Adorf, a celebrated actor known for his roles in 'Tin Drum' and 'Winnetou,' has passed away at the age of 95. His contributions to European cinema have left a lasting legacy.

item.title: Amazon Deals of the Day: Get Your Pool Ready With a Record Low on the Aiper Scuba S1 Robotic Pool Cleaner
item.description: Amazon is offering significant discounts on various products, including a record low on the Aiper Scuba S1 robotic pool cleaner and discounts on Ray-Ban Meta glasses and Blink security cameras. These deals aim to attract consumers preparing for summer.

item.title: Motorola Razr 70 Ultra surfaces in new leak following Razr Fold release
item.description: Leaks about the Motorola Razr 70 Ultra reveal improved specifications, including better battery life, as it prepares for launch later this year. This follows the recent release of the Razr Fold.

item.title: tensor-optix 1.2.5
item.description: Tensor-optix 1.2.5 has been released, providing an autonomous training loop for sequential learning models with built-in algorithms for TensorFlow and PyTorch. This update aims to simplify the training process for developers.

item.title: Nvidia’s New PC Strategy Is Bigger Than a Single Acquisition
item.description: Nvidia is focusing on strategic partnerships and licensing deals rather than acquisitions to strengthen its position in the PC market. Collaborations with Groq, Intel, and MediaTek are central to this strategy.

item.title: Building an Autonomous Travel Assistant with Copilot Studio
item.description: A new project using Copilot Studio allows users to build an autonomous travel assistant that can answer questions, provide safety advice, and plan trips. This no-code solution aims to make travel planning more accessible.

item.title: Save $50 Off the Latest Apple AirPods Pro 3 Noise Canceling Earbuds at Amazon
item.description: Amazon is offering a $50 discount on the latest Apple AirPods Pro 3, known for their noise-canceling features. This promotion highlights the popularity of high-quality audio products among consumers.
